name: SEagenthub
description: A portable 6-agent MCP server covering the full SDLC and AWS Well-Architected Framework. Runs architect → codeCrafter → codeReviewer → qualityGuard → devOps in a step-and-wait pipeline with human-in-the-loop deployment approval.

tools.name: techLead
tools.description: Start the 6-agent SDLC pipeline on a local project directory. Runs the architect agent first and returns a STEP_COMPLETE response with proposed_changes and a next_agent_instruction. The IDE must write the files to disk, then call advance_pipeline.

project_path.description: Absolute local path to the project directory.

task_description.description: Plain-English description of what the pipeline should accomplish.

tools.name: advance_pipeline
tools.description: Advance to the next agent step. Call this only after writing all files from the previous proposed_changes to disk. If any required file is missing, the server returns WAITING_FOR_FILES and does not advance.

continuation_token.description: The continuation_token from the previous STEP_COMPLETE response.

tools.name: resume_refactor_decision
tools.description: Resume a pipeline paused at the refactor permission gate. Use the thread_id from the PIPELINE_PAUSED message and answer Yes or No to the proposed out-of-scope refactor.

thread_id.description: The thread_id returned in the PIPELINE_PAUSED message.

decision.description: Yes to approve the refactor (adds it as an active sub-task). No to skip and continue.

tools.name: resume_deployment_decision
tools.description: Resume a pipeline paused at the deployment approval gate. Use the thread_id from the PIPELINE_PAUSED message and choose Approve (automated deploy) or Manual (generate a deployment guide).

thread_id.description: The thread_id returned in the PIPELINE_PAUSED message.

decision.description: Approve to run the full automated devOps pipeline. Manual to generate docs/deployment_guide.md for manual execution.

runtimes.description: Local execution via Claude Code or any MCP-compatible IDE.
AWS Lambda via Lambda Web Adapter — HTTP requests forwarded to FastMCP on port 8080.

DEPLOY_DASHBOARD_URL.description: HTTP POST endpoint for the deployment dashboard. Receives status events from the devOps agent.

PORT.description: HTTP port for streamable-http and SSE transports. Defaults to 8080. Set automatically by the Lambda Web Adapter.
